# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3' #specifying version of docker compose 
services:   #defining services in this section, each service represents a container running on one or more servers(elk-stack) as per requirement mentioned above for centralized logging platform. Here we define only three different types (log collection/storage and visualization), but it can be extended to include other components like metrics etc if required 
  
  elk_stack: #defining a service named "elk" which is the docker container running all of ELK Stack's services. Else, when you access using curl or similar tool in your local machine via browser it may not work as expected due to port exposure (access Kibana UI and elasticsearch ports).
    image: elk-stack #setting base Image 
  	ports:                   #defining the exposed docker services for ELK stack's logs, storage etc. here we expose ElasticSearch on Port 9201(HTTP) , Logstash/Elastic Search port is also set to be same as logging system (Port 5678), and Kibana UI at a specific hostname with the PORT defined
      - "9200:9200"   #Exposing Elastisearch on this Port    http://localhost:[PORT]:[SERVICE_EXPORTS]/_.json  or [http-host][port]-/_search?q=stackoverflow&format=json and replace PORT with your actual port number
      - "5678:5678"   #Exposing Logstash Port, here we are using same as logging system from above. http://localhost:[PORT]:[SERVICE_EXPORTS]/index  or [http-host][port]-/_search?q=stackoverflow&format=json and replace PORT with your actual port number
      - "9001:9000"   #Exposing Kibana UI Port, here we are using same as logging system from above. http://localhost:[PORT]:[SERVICE_EXPORTS]/_plugin/kibana//  or [http-host][port]-/_search?q=stackoverflow&format=json and replace PORT with your actual port number
    environment:             #defining the Environment variables for all ELK Stack's services, here we set up Logging system using centralized log files. This is a common way to provide configuration details at runtime while running containers  in docker environments like Java/JEE applications or microservices with multiple instances of them
      - ELASTICSEARCH_URL=http://elasticsearch:9201   #providing elastic search URL on which logging system will work. here we set up Logging System using centralized log files at runtime in the docker container environment while running containers, so far I have not provided any configuration file or details for them as per requirement mentioned above
      - LOGSTASH_URL=http://logstash:5678   #providing logs and events capture port URL on which LogStas uses log parsing. here we set up the same using centralized logging system  in docker environment while running containers, so far I have not provided any configuration file or details for them as per requirement mentioned above
      - KIBANA_URL=http://kibana:9001   #providing url of kibna UI port on which ElasticSearch and Logstash are connected. here we set up the same using centralized logging system in docker environment while running containers, so far I have not provided any configuration file or details for them as per requirement mentioned above
